content.problemStatement:
You have an array AAA of size NNN, and a complete weighted graph with NNN nodes and N⋅(N−1)2\frac{N \cdot (N-1)}{2}2N⋅(N−1)​ undirected weighted edges connecting each pair of nodes.

The weight of the edge between node iii and node jjj is ∣j−i∣⋅max⁡(Ai,Aj)|j - i| \cdot \max(A_i, A_j)∣j−i∣⋅max(Ai​,Aj​).

The distance between 222 nodes is defined as the **minimum sum** of weights on a path connecting the 222 nodes.

Find the distance between nodes 111 and NNN.

Note that ∣x∣|x|∣x∣ denotes the absolute value of xxx. For example, ∣−7∣=7|-7| = 7∣−7∣=7 and ∣4∣=4|4| = 4∣4∣=4.


Input Format

The first line of input will contain a single integer TTT, denoting the number of test cases.
Each test case consists of multiple lines of input.

The first line of each test case contains a single integer NNN, the number of nodes in the graph and the size of array AAA.
The second line of each test case contains NNN integers, A1,A2,…,ANA_1, A_2, \ldots, A_NA1​,A2​,…,AN​.




Output Format
For each test case, output on a new line, the distance between nodes 111 and NNN.
constraints: The sum of NNN over all test cases does not exceed 2⋅1052 \cdot 10^52⋅105.

sampleTestCases.explanation:
Explanation:
Test case 111: The optimal path is 1→21 \rightarrow 21→2 and then 2→42 \rightarrow 42→4 with sum of weights 2+2=42 + 2 = 42+2=4.
Test case 222: The optimal path is 1→21 \rightarrow 21→2 and then 2→32 \rightarrow 32→3 with sum of weights 3+2=53 + 2 = 53+2=5.
